title: Packet ownership semantics clearly documented
description:
The codebase consistently documents packet ownership semantics:

**lichen_l2.h header comment (lines 15-20):**
```c
/*
 * Packet ownership (Zephyr net_l2 contract):
 * - TX (L2 send): On success (return 0), L2 takes ownership of net_pkt and
 *   calls net_pkt_unref(). On error (return < 0), caller retains ownership.
 * - RX (lichen_l2_input): Allocates a new net_pkt and passes ownership to
 *   the network stack via net_recv_data().
 */
```

**lichen_l2_send (lines 298-301):**
```c
/*
 * Ownership: Per Zephyr net_l2 contract, on success (return 0) this function
 * takes ownership of @p pkt and calls net_pkt_unref(). On error (return < 0),
 * caller retains ownership and is responsible for cleanup.
 */
```

**lichen_l2_input (lines 848-856):**
```c
/*
 * Inject into the network stack.
 *
 * Ownership semantics:
 * - On success (ret >= 0): net_recv_data takes ownership of pkt.
 *   The network stack will unref the packet when processing completes.
 *   We MUST NOT access or unref pkt after this point.
 * - On failure (ret < 0): We retain ownership and must unref pkt.
 */
```

This documentation prevents use-after-free and double-free bugs that are notoriously hard to debug in networking code.
close_reason: Positive finding - no action needed, documents good practice
